title: Forgotten
description:
Minecraft Classic with RPG Mechanics, Meat-Based Tech, and Multiplayer Compatible Hardcore. 
The journey to remembering is not for the feint of heart.
body:
![Ruins and Renewal Title Card](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/t0mg3DmM/images/aec29e501dad931bc8030de5b73e1668fb2b85a2.png)


What if Minecraft was developed as an RPG?, What if it was entirely focused on challenge, what if it forced you to slow down, stop speed running through your burnout phase, and finally take the time to enjoy minecraft again?... Personally I wouldn't know, but maybe try out Forgotten? Pretty please???

![A Foreign Object in a Familiar Land](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/t0mg3DmM/images/9ae83127f5c459d7d5b12a9373a840bd9de05aa2.png)
A Hardcore Challenge RPG Modpack, designed to be experienced and learned alongside friends. Start with barely your wits about you and learn what the world has to offer as if still experiencing your first fresh playthrough of Minecraft. So much awaits you as you grow and learn, slowly unlocking new features, items, and paths, Forgotten is the way of old, but here you will remember it.

![A Small Camp in the Darkness](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/t0mg3DmM/images/d91766f5eca33a43e17285f4cdd25803fdbafd8e.png)
Forgotten currently (as of 1.2.0) has 107 unique quests and works well as an SMP Modpack even in spite of it being Hardcore thanks to mods like Hardcore Revival, and Hardcore Redeploy keeping players in the game so long as a few of you are prepared enough to do so. 

Many vanilla features are reworked such as mending and totems of undying both being removed in leu of the new harcore revival systems and the more punishing blood-mending enchantment. Forgotten aims to be a Fun Challenge, a balanced Multiplayer Experience, and a Cure to Minecraft Burnout!
